source_pr.title: planner: Solve the problem that the parent query condition is pushed incorrectly when the setvar function is evaluated in the subquery.（#11622）
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
fix issue: #11622  when the setvar function is evaluated in the subquery, the parent query condition is pushed down wrongly.


### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Integration test

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select t01.c1,t01.c2,t01.c3 from (select t1.*,@c3:=@c3+1 as c3 from (select t.*,@c3:=0 from t order by t.c1)t1)t01 where t01.c3=1 and t01.c2='d'").Check(testkit.Rows()) (planner/core/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR fixes predicate-pushdown correctness with subquery `setvar` evaluation; no concrete intermittent flaky signature is provided in the case data.
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR is a logical correctness fix (predicate pushdown behavior when `setvar` is evaluated in a subquery). The case data does not include a concrete intermittent failure signature or a targeted stabilization fix pattern, so keep it as needs-more-evidence rather than guessing the root cause taxonomy.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select t01.c1,t01.c2,t01.c3 from (select t1.*,@c3:=@c3+1 as c3 from (select t.*,@c3:=0 from t order by t.c1)t1)t01 where t01.c3=1 and t01.c2='d'").Check(testkit.Rows()) (planner/core/integration_test.go)
